html:
[1265]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宴于未央 - Feasting at Weiyang, referring to a banquet held at the Weiyang Palace, a significant imperial residence in Han Dynasty China.
  • 忽闻人语 - Suddenly heard someone speaking, an idiomatic expression describing an unexpected auditory experience.
  • 缘柱而下 - Climbing down the column, a vivid description of the old man's movement.
  • 放杖稽首 - Laying down his staff and bowing deeply, a formal gesture of respect or supplication.
  • 嘿而不言 - Silent and not speaking, indicating the old man's initial reluctance to communicate.
  • 因仰头视屋 - Then he looked up at the roof, a metaphorical action that requires interpretation.
  • 俯指帝脚 - Then he pointed down at the emperor's feet, another metaphorical action.
  • 忽然不见 - Suddenly disappeared, indicating the old man's mysterious departure.
  • 骇愕不知何等 - Startled and not knowing what kind of thing it was, expressing the emperor's confusion and surprise.
  • 东方朔必识之 - Dongfang Shuo must know about it, a reference to the famous scholar and court jester Dongfang Shuo.
  • 其名为藻 - His name is Ao, introducing the identity of the old man.
  • 水木之精 - The spirit of water and wood, a mystical description of the old man's nature.
  • 夏巢幽林 - In summer, he nests in deep forests, describing the old man's habitat.
  • 冬潜深河 - In winter, he hides in deep rivers, further describing the old man's habitat.
  • 陛下顷日，频兴造宫室 - Recently, Your Majesty has frequently built palaces, referring to the emperor's construction activities.
  • 故来诉耳 - So he came to complain, explaining the old man's reason for appearing.
  • 足也 - Feet, a metaphorical reference to the old man's message.
  • 愿陛下宫室足于此 - I hope Your Majesty's palaces are sufficient here, the old man's plea to stop further construction.
  • 帝感之，既而息役 - The emperor was moved by this and stopped the construction, indicating the outcome of the encounter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, one of the most famous emperors of the Han Dynasty, known for his military conquests and cultural patronage.
  • 未央 - The Weiyang Palace, a grand imperial palace in Chang'an (modern Xi'an), where many important events took place during the Han Dynasty.
  • 东方朔 - Dongfang Shuo, a famous scholar, court jester, and advisor to Emperor Wu of Han, known for his wit and wisdom.
  • 水木之精 - A mystical concept in Chinese folklore, referring to spirits or beings associated with natural elements.
  • 夏巢幽林，冬潜深河 - Descriptions of the old man's habitat, reflecting the seasonal cycles and natural environment of ancient China.
  • 频兴造宫室 - Refers to the emperor's frequent construction projects, which were common during the Han Dynasty as part of imperial grandeur and expansion.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 未央 - Weiyang Palace.
  • 东方朔 - Dongfang Shuo.
  • 藻 - Ao, the name of the old man.

[1266]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幸瓠子河 - To visit the Hukou River, where '幸' indicates a royal visit or journey.
  • 水底有弦歌声 - Literally 'music from the bottom of the water,' suggesting an otherworldly or supernatural occurrence.
  • 梁上翁 - Refers to an old man on the beam, likely a supernatural being or spirit.
  • 年少数人 - Means 'a few young people,' emphasizing their youthfulness.
  • 绛衣素带 - Describes clothing with red robes and white belts, indicating a formal or ceremonial attire.
  • 缨佩甚鲜 - Means 'ornaments and decorations are very bright,' highlighting the vivid appearance of the beings.
  • 凌波而出 - Literally 'emerging from the waves,' describing a graceful or supernatural emergence.
  • 衣不沾濡 - Means 'clothes not wet,' suggesting the beings are unaffected by the water.
  • 挟乐器者 - Means 'those carrying musical instruments,' indicating they are prepared to perform.
  • 帝方食 - Means 'the emperor was eating,' setting the scene during a meal.
  • 为之辍膳 - Means 'causing him to stop eating,' showing the impact of the supernatural event.
  • 命列坐于食案前 - Means 'ordered them to sit in a row in front of the food table,' indicating a formal arrangement.
  • 奏乐 - Means 'to play music,' a common term for musical performance.
  • 赍乐来 - Means 'brought their music with them,' indicating they were prepared to perform.
  • 弦而歌 - Means 'to play and sing,' describing the act of musical performance.
  • 安敢不奏 - Means 'how dare we not perform,' expressing a sense of duty or obligation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瓠子河 - Refers to the Hukou River, a specific location in Chinese history.
  • 帝 - Refers to the emperor, a central figure in Chinese imperial culture.
  • 梁上翁 - Likely refers to a supernatural being or spirit, common in Chinese folklore and mythology.
  • 绛衣素带 - Describes formal or ceremonial attire, often associated with deities or spirits.
  • 缨佩 - Refers to ornaments and decorations, often used to describe the attire of deities or spirits.
  • 凌波 - A term often used in Chinese poetry to describe a graceful or supernatural movement.
  • 衣不沾濡 - Suggests the beings are supernatural, as they are unaffected by the water.
  • 挟乐器者 - Indicates the beings are prepared to perform, suggesting a formal or ceremonial context.
  • 帝方食 - Sets the scene during a meal, a common context for supernatural encounters in Chinese literature.
  • 为之辍膳 - Shows the impact of the supernatural event, causing the emperor to stop eating.
  • 命列坐于食案前 - Indicates a formal arrangement, suggesting a ceremonial or ritual context.
  • 奏乐 - A common term for musical performance, often associated with rituals or ceremonies.
  • 赍乐来 - Indicates the beings brought their music with them, suggesting a prepared or formal performance.
  • 弦而歌 - Describes the act of musical performance, often associated with rituals or ceremonies.

Proper Nouns

  • 瓠子河 - The Hukou River, a specific location in Chinese history.
  • 帝 - The emperor, a central figure in Chinese imperial culture.
  • 梁上翁 - An old man on the beam, likely a supernatural being or spirit.
  • 年少数人 - A few young people, emphasizing their youthfulness.
  • 绛衣素带 - Describes formal or ceremonial attire, often associated with deities or spirits.
  • 缨佩 - Refers to ornaments and decorations, often used to describe the attire of deities or spirits.
  • 凌波 - A term often used in Chinese poetry to describe a graceful or supernatural movement.
  • 衣不沾濡 - Suggests the beings are supernatural, as they are unaffected by the water.
  • 挟乐器者 - Indicates the beings are prepared to perform, suggesting a formal or ceremonial context.
  • 帝方食 - Sets the scene during a meal, a common context for supernatural encounters in Chinese literature.
  • 为之辍膳 - Shows the impact of the supernatural event, causing the emperor to stop eating.
  • 命列坐于食案前 - Indicates a formal arrangement, suggesting a ceremonial or ritual context.
  • 奏乐 - A common term for musical performance, often associated with rituals or ceremonies.
  • 赍乐来 - Indicates the beings brought their music with them, suggesting a prepared or formal performance.
  • 弦而歌 - Describes the act of musical performance, often associated with rituals or ceremonies.

[1267]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天地德兮垂至仁 - Classical expression invoking the benevolence of Heaven and Earth. '德' (virtue) and '仁' (benevolence) are key Confucian concepts.
  • 悯幽魄兮停斧斤 - '悯' (pity) and '幽魄' (the souls of the dead) evoke compassion for the deceased. '停斧斤' (halt the axes) is a metaphor for stopping the destruction of tombs or graves.
  • 保窟宅兮庇微身 - '保' (protect), '窟宅' (dwellings), and '庇' (shelter) emphasize the protection of the humble and the dead.
  • 愿天子兮寿万春 - A wish for the emperor's longevity, with '寿万春' (live ten thousand springs) being a hyperbolic expression of eternal life.
  • 歌声小大，无异于人 - '无异于人' (no different from others) suggests that the singing is comparable to human voices.
  • 清彻绕越梁栋 - '清彻' (clear and pure) and '绕越梁栋' (echoing through beams and pillars) describe the quality and spread of the music.
  • 调契声谐 - '调契' (in harmony) and '声谐' (sounds are in accord) emphasize the perfect tuning and coordination of the music.
  • 举觞并劝曰 - '举觞' (raise the cup) and '并劝' (together encourage) describe the act of sharing a drink and offering congratulations.
  • 不德不足当雅贶 - '不德' (not worthy of) and '雅贶' (elegant gift) express humility in accepting the gift.
  • 献帝一紫螺壳 - '献帝' (present to the emperor) and '紫螺壳' (purple snail shell) describe the gift.
  • 是蛟龙髓 - '蛟龙髓' (dragon's marrow) is a mythical substance with special properties.
  • 以傅面，令人好颜色 - '以傅面' (apply to the face) and '令人好颜色' (make one look beautiful) describe the cosmetic use of the dragon's marrow.
  • 又女子在孕，产之必易 - '产之必易' (easier childbirth) describes the medicinal use of the dragon's marrow.
  • 何以此珠名洞穴珠 - '何以' (why) and '名' (named) ask for the reason behind the pearl's name.
  • 河底有一穴，深数百丈 - '河底' (river bottom) and '深数百丈' (hundreds of feet deep) describe the location and depth of the cave.
  • 中有赤蚌，蚌生珠 - '中有赤蚌' (contains red clams) and '蚌生珠' (clams produce pearls) describe the origin of the pearl.
  • 帝既深叹此事，又服朔之奇识 - '深叹此事' (deeply admire this matter) and '服朔之奇识' (admire the extraordinary knowledge of the scholar) express the emperor's appreciation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天地德兮垂至仁 - Invokes the Confucian concept of Heaven and Earth's benevolence.
  • 悯幽魄兮停斧斤 - References the ancient practice of stopping the destruction of tombs to show respect for the dead.
  • 保窟宅兮庇微身 - Emphasizes the protection of the humble and the dead, reflecting Confucian values.
  • 愿天子兮寿万春 - A common wish for the emperor's longevity, reflecting the importance of the emperor in Chinese culture.
  • 歌声小大，无异于人 - Describes the singing as comparable to human voices, highlighting the quality of the music.
  • 清彻绕越梁栋 - Describes the clear and pure music echoing through the palace, emphasizing the beauty and grandeur of the event.
  • 调契声谐 - Describes the perfect tuning and coordination of the music, reflecting the importance of harmony in Chinese aesthetics.
  • 举觞并劝曰 - Describes the act of sharing a drink and offering congratulations, reflecting the social and ceremonial aspects of the event.
  • 不德不足当雅贶 - Expresses humility in accepting the gift, reflecting Confucian values of modesty.
  • 献帝一紫螺壳 - Describes the gift of a purple snail shell, which contains a special substance.
  • 是蛟龙髓 - References the mythical substance of dragon's marrow, which has special properties.
  • 以傅面，令人好颜色 - Describes the cosmetic use of the dragon's marrow, reflecting the importance of appearance in Chinese culture.
  • 又女子在孕，产之必易 - Describes the medicinal use of the dragon's marrow, reflecting the importance of health and well-being in Chinese culture.
  • 何以此珠名洞穴珠 - Asks for the reason behind the pearl's name, reflecting the curiosity and interest in natural phenomena.
  • 河底有一穴，深数百丈 - Describes the location and depth of the cave, reflecting the importance of natural wonders in Chinese culture.
  • 中有赤蚌，蚌生珠 - Describes the origin of the pearl, reflecting the importance of nature and its resources in Chinese culture.
  • 帝既深叹此事，又服朔之奇识 - Expresses the emperor's appreciation for the extraordinary knowledge of the scholar, reflecting the value placed on wisdom and learning in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 天子 - Refers to the emperor.
  • 紫螺壳 - Refers to a purple snail shell.
  • 蛟龙髓 - Refers to the mythical substance of dragon's marrow.
  • 洞穴珠 - Refers to the pearl found in the cave.
  • 河底 - Refers to the river bottom.
  • 赤蚌 - Refers to red clams.
  • 帝 - Refers to the emperor.
  • 朔 - Refers to the scholar or advisor.

[1268]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舒大才 - Proper noun, transliterated as 'Shu Dacai' to maintain consistency.
  • 云问之逸士也 - Refers to 'Yunwen, a reclusive scholar,' indicating a person who lives a secluded and intellectual life.
  • 聪慧能文，尤长于诗 - Describes the subject's literary talents, particularly in poetry.
  • 麒德二年春 - Refers to the second year of the Qide era during the spring season, transliterated as 'Qide.'
  • 驾舟访友 - Describes a leisurely journey, a common motif in classical Chinese literature.
  • 抵中途 - Indicates the journey was interrupted, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.
  • 天已薄暮 - Describes the time of day, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 时闻大鱼跳掷于波间 - Describes the sounds of the environment, a common sensory detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 宿鸟飞鸣于岸际 - Describes the sounds of the environment, a common sensory detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 云散月明 - Describes a clear night sky, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 花香柳舞 - Describes a pleasant environment, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 忽兰麝风透，环佩铿锵 - Describes the presence of a graceful and elegant figure, a common motif in classical Chinese literature.
  • 大才异之 - Indicates the subject's surprise, a common reaction in classical Chinese literature.
  • 蛟舟谛视 - Describes the subject's cautious approach, a common action in classical Chinese literature.
  • 一美人姿容妍丽 - Describes a beautiful woman, a common character in classical Chinese literature.
  • 偕二婢嬉游于林下 - Describes the setting and company of the woman, a common scene in classical Chinese literature.
  • 生乃登岸揖曰 - Describes the subject's polite introduction, a common action in classical Chinese literature.
  • 娘子高居何处，夜行至此 - Describes the subject's question, a common dialogue in classical Chinese literature.
  • 敝居僻陋，离此咫尺 - Describes the woman's response, a common dialogue in classical Chinese literature.
  • 君如不鄙，枉驾一顾 - Describes the woman's invitation, a common dialogue in classical Chinese literature.
  • 大才情动于心中 - Describes the subject's emotional response, a common reaction in classical Chinese literature.
  • 不得已，遂与美人先后而行 - Describes the subject's decision, a common action in classical Chinese literature.
  • 不半里许 - Describes the distance traveled, a common detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 遥见竹户荆扉，花木掩映 - Describes the woman's home, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 明窗净几，亦甚整洁 - Describes the neatness of the home, a common detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 美人逊生上坐 - Describes the woman's hospitality, a common action in classical Chinese literature.
  • 命侍姬献茶，继以酒馔 - Describes the hospitality provided, a common action in classical Chinese literature.
  • 杯盘精致，非世所有 - Describes the quality of the items served, a common detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 壁间挂四时回文诗四绝 - Describes the decorations in the home, a common detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 美人自制也 - Describes the authorship of the poems, a common detail in classical Chinese literature.
  • 其曰 - Introduces the content of the poems, a common literary device in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

[1269]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 花艳吐枝红傍雨 - Poetic description of flowers blooming with red petals amidst the rain.
  • 柳细垂绿绦迎风 - Poetic description of willows with thin, green branches swaying in the wind.
  • 霞生远汉东升日 - Poetic description of the sunrise, with the sun rising in the east and casting a colorful glow.
  • 月落闲窗北近松 - Poetic description of the moon setting, with its light illuminating a window and nearby pine trees.
  • 凉生高阁虚檐冷 - Poetic description of a cool breeze coming from a high tower with an open eave.
  • 齿嚼冰丝雪藕寒 - Poetic description of eating cold, refreshing foods like ice silk and snow lotus root.
  • 香散榴花红灼灼 - Poetic description of the fragrance of pomegranate flowers spreading widely.
  • 露倾荷叶翠团团 - Poetic description of dew droplets on green lotus leaves.
  • 芦覆岸深秋水碧 - Poetic description of reeds covering the riverbank with deep, clear blue water in autumn.
  • 木凋霜凛晓天苍 - Poetic description of trees shedding their leaves under the cold frost of dawn, with a clear blue sky.
  • 孤眠夜永愁空馆 - Poetic description of solitary sleep in a long, empty room at night.
  • 独立朝长远乡 - Poetic description of standing alone in the distance, looking towards a faraway hometown.
  • 天堕雪花冰满户 - Poetic description of snow falling from the sky, covering the house with ice.
  • 雨飞风冽冻凝城 - Poetic description of rain and cold winds freezing the city.
  • 鲜鲜蕊绽梅容瘦 - Poetic description of fresh, delicate petals blooming on a thin, elegant plum blossom.
  • 滴滴香倾酒味清 - Poetic description of the fragrance of wine dripping down, with a clear, refreshing taste.
  • 美人遽曰 - Poetic description of a beautiful woman speaking suddenly.
  • 效颦鄙句 - Poetic description of trying to imitate something, but doing it poorly or with a sense of humility.
  • 愧无好词 - Poetic description of feeling ashamed for not having good words.
  • 大才称赞不容口 - Poetic description of a talented person praising someone without being able to stop.
  • 询以姓名居址 - Poetic description of asking for someone's name and address.
  • 蕊真，小字也 - Poetic description of a nickname, '蕊真.'
  • 律说大法 - Poetic description of laws and major principles.
  • 礼顺人情 - Poetic description of rituals and customs that follow human emotions.
  • 之螽斯传声 - Poetic description of the sound of cicadas being transmitted.
  • 之草虫即应 - Poetic description of grasshoppers responding to the sound.
  • 何以人而不如微物乎 - Poetic description of questioning why humans cannot be as simple and natural as small creatures.
  • 大才乃援笔而和之 - Poetic description of a talented person taking up the pen to write a response.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花艳吐枝红傍雨 - Refers to the beauty of flowers in a rainy setting.
  • 柳细垂绿绦迎风 - Refers to the grace of willows in the wind.
  • 霞生远汉东升日 - Refers to the sunrise, a common theme in Chinese poetry.
  • 月落闲窗北近松 - Refers to the moon setting, often used to evoke a sense of solitude and tranquility.
  • 凉生高阁虚檐冷 - Refers to a high, open-roofed building, often associated with a sense of coolness and seclusion.
  • 齿嚼冰丝雪藕寒 - Refers to eating cold, refreshing foods, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 香散榴花红灼灼 - Refers to the fragrance of pomegranate flowers, a symbol of beauty and vitality.
  • 露倾荷叶翠团团 - Refers to dew on lotus leaves, a symbol of purity and clarity.
  • 芦覆岸深秋水碧 - Refers to the autumn season, with reeds covering the riverbank and clear blue water.
  • 木凋霜凛晓天苍 - Refers to the cold frost of dawn, with trees shedding their leaves and a clear blue sky.
  • 孤眠夜永愁空馆 - Refers to solitary sleep in a long, empty room, a common theme in Chinese poetry.
  • 独立朝长远乡 - Refers to standing alone in the distance, looking towards a faraway hometown, a common theme in Chinese poetry.
  • 天堕雪花冰满户 - Refers to a snowy day, with snow covering the house with ice.
  • 雨飞风冽冻凝城 - Refers to a cold, rainy day, with the city frozen.
  • 鲜鲜蕊绽梅容瘦 - Refers to the blooming of plum blossoms, a symbol of resilience and beauty.
  • 滴滴香倾酒味清 - Refers to the fragrance of wine, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 美人遽曰 - Refers to a beautiful woman speaking suddenly, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 效颦鄙句 - Refers to the act of imitating something poorly, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 愧无好词 - Refers to feeling ashamed for not having good words, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 大才称赞不容口 - Refers to a talented person praising someone without being able to stop, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 询以姓名居址 - Refers to asking for someone's name and address, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 蕊真，小字也 - Refers to a nickname, '蕊真,' a common theme in Chinese literature.

  • 礼顺人情 - Refers to rituals and customs that follow human emotions, a common theme in Chinese literature.

  • 之草虫即应 - Refers to grasshoppers responding to the sound, a common theme in Chinese literature.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '上马腾腾而行，倏忽渐高，但讶其稳疾，不自知其云上也' must be read as a continuous description of the rider's experience, emphasizing the sudden and rapid ascent.
  • The elliptical phrase '随所跃，辄滴之' must be understood as the horse's movements causing raindrops to fall.
  • The sequence '电掣云开，下见所憩村' describes a sudden change in the weather and the rider's view from above.
  • The sequence '吾扰此村多矣，方德其人，计无以报' expresses the rider's regret and sense of indebtedness.
  • The sequence '今久旱，苗稼将悴，而雨在我手，宁复惜之' describes the current situation and the rider's resolve to help.
  • The sequence '顾一滴不足濡，乃连下二十滴' shows the rider's action to provide sufficient rain.

  • 忽达于洲岛间 - Describes the sudden arrival at an island. '忽' means 'suddenly' or 'unexpectedly.'
  • 瞀然具以事告 - The subject replied in a dazed or confused manner, providing a full account of the events. '瞀然' means 'confused' or 'dazed.'

  • 吾闻，魂气升于天，形魄归于地，是无质矣，何鬼之有 - Statement meaning 'I have heard that the spirit rises to heaven, and the body returns to earth. Therefore, there is no substance, so how can there be a ghost?' Argues that the singer cannot be a ghost because it lacks a physical form.
  • 佞人也 - Term meaning 'a cunning or deceitful person.' Describes the nature of a fox.
  • 一中其媚，祸必能及 - Statement meaning 'If one falls for its charm, disaster will surely follow.' Warns against the dangers of being deceived by a cunning entity.
  • 其世业功德，实利生民 - Statement meaning 'Its family's virtue and merit truly benefit the people.' Argues that the singer's lineage has done good for humanity.
  • 某虽不淑，焉能苟媚而欲奉祸乎 - Statement meaning 'Although I am not virtuous, how could I flatter and bring disaster?' Expresses the singer's refusal to be deceitful.

  • 复以德王 - Statement meaning 'He was then made king by virtue.' Describes the ancestor's elevation to a position of power.
  • 神农陶唐氏，后又王于西汉 - Statement meaning 'Shennong and Taotang, later became kings in the Western Han dynasty.' Describes the lineage's connection to historical figures.
  • 因食采于宋，远祖无忌，以威猛暴耗，人不可亲，遂为白泽氏所执 - Statement meaning 'Because of their fief in Song, our distant ancestor Wuji, due to his fierce and violent nature, was not approachable and was captured by Bai Ze.' Describes the ancestor's downfall.
  • 汉明帝时，佛法东流 - Statement meaning 'During the reign of Emperor Ming of Han, Buddhism spread to the East.' Provides historical context.
  • 摩竺法，兰二罗汉，奏请某十四代祖，令显扬释教 - Statement meaning 'Two Arhats, Mofu and Lan, petitioned for my fourteenth-generation ancestor to promote Buddhism.' Describes the ancestor's role in spreading Buddhism.
  • 魏武季年灭佛法、诛道士，而长明公幽死 - Statement meaning 'In the later years of Cao Cao, he suppressed Buddhism and killed Daoists, and the Longming Gong died in obscurity.' Describes the persecution of Buddhism.
  • 魏文嗣位，佛法重兴，复以长明世子袭之 - Statement meaning 'When Wei Wen ascended the throne, Buddhism revived, and the title of Longming Gong was inherited by his son.' Describes the revival of Buddhism.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 誰怜潘果空投，贾香难与，愁肠安托 - Complex phrase expressing sorrow and longing. '谁怜' (shéi lián) means 'who pities,' '潘果空投' (pān guǒ kōng tóu) is unclear but likely refers to a metaphorical or symbolic gesture, '贾香难与' (jiǎ xiāng nán yǔ) suggests difficulty in sharing or communicating, and '愁肠安托' (chóu cháng ān tuō) means 'where to entrust one's sorrowful heart.'
  • 带眼轻拴，须看取杨柳腰肢如削 - '带眼轻拴' (dài yǎn qīng shuān) means 'lightly fastened with a belt,' '须看取' (xū kàn qǔ) means 'must see,' '杨柳腰肢如削' (yáng liǔ yāo zhī rú xuē) is a poetic description of a slender waist like that of willow.
  • 珠履玲珑，罗衫雅淡，件件无心着 - '珠履玲珑' (zhū lǚ líng lóng) means 'jeweled shoes that are delicate,' '罗衫雅淡' (luó shān yǎ dàn) means 'silk robes that are elegant and subdued,' '件件无心着' (jiàn jiàn wú xīn zhe) means 'each piece is worn without thought.'
  • 何时厮见，得偿今日萧索 - '何时厮见' (hé shí sī jiàn) means 'when will we meet again,' '得偿今日萧索' (dé cháng jīn rì xiāo suǒ) means 'to make up for today's loneliness.'
  • 花径争穿，珠帘屡认，正逢梅雨芹泥润 - '花径争穿' (huā jìng zhēng chuān) means 'competing to walk through the flower path,' '珠帘屡认' (zhū lián lǚ rèn) means 'often recognizing the pearl curtains,' '正逢梅雨芹泥润' (zhèng féng méi yǔ qín ní rùn) means 'just encountering the rainy season when the soil is moist.'
  • 画梁无处可安巢，玉纤为把花枝衬 - '画梁无处可安巢' (huà liáng wú chù kě ān cháo) means 'there is no place to build a nest on the painted beam,' '玉纤为把花枝衬' (yù xiān wèi bǎ huā zhī chèn) means 'the delicate hands hold up the flower branches.'
  • 社日才来，端阳已近，寻巢为甚偏迟钝 - '社日才来' (shè rì cái lái) means 'the *Shè Rì* festival has just arrived,' '端阳已近' (duān yáng yǐ jìn) means 'the *Duān Yáng* festival is approaching,' '寻巢为甚偏迟钝' (xún cháo wéi shén piān chí dùn) means 'why is finding a nest so slow?'
  • 算来一似凤鸾期，跎蛇渐觉无真信 - '算来一似凤鸾期' (suàn lái yī sì fèng luán qī) means 'it seems like the time of the phoenix and crane,' '跎蛇渐觉无真信' (tuó shé jiàn jué wú zhēn xìn) means 'the snake gradually feels no true sign.'
  • 昨夜惊眠梅雨大，枕前窗上频敲，天明翻觉梦魂遥 - '昨夜惊眠梅雨大' (zuó yè jīng mián méi yǔ dà) means 'last night the heavy rain woke me from sleep,' '枕前窗上频敲' (zhěn qián chuāng shàng pín qiāo) means 'the raindrops frequently tap on the pillow and window,' '天明翻觉梦魂遥' (tiān míng fān jué mèng hún yáo) means 'at dawn, I realize my dream was far away.'
  • 起来看女伴，薰袖已香消 - '起来看女伴' (qǐ lái kàn nǚ bàn) means 'get up to see my female companions,' '薰袖已香消' (xūn xiù yǐ xiāng xiāo) means 'the fragrant sleeves have already faded.'
  • 云锁房栊烟锁竹，卷帘水湿鲛绡 - '云锁房栊烟锁竹' (yún suǒ fáng lóng yān suǒ zhú) means 'clouds lock the windows, smoke locks the bamboo,' '卷帘水湿鲛绡' (juǎn lián shuǐ shī jiāo xiāo) means 'the water wets the curtains.'
  • 菱花低照拂眉梢，玉梳云发润，不喜上兰膏 - '菱花低照拂眉梢' (líng huā dī zhào fú méi shāo) means 'the dim light of the mirror reflects on the eyebrows,' '玉梳云发润' (yù shū yún fā rùn) means 'the jade comb smooths the cloud-like hair,' '不喜上兰膏' (bù xǐ shàng lán gāo) means 'I don't like applying the fragrant oil.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '闻已，归告奴仆，乘马便走' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The phrase '衣服悉弃于厅中' is elliptical but clear in meaning.
  • The long sequence '至夜，已行五六十里，出县界，止宿村店' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The clause '仆从但怪奔走，不知何故' can be read as two linked ideas: the servants were surprised by the guest's actions and did not know the reason.
  • The sequence '此人歇定，乃言此贼负心之状，言讫吁嗟，奴仆悉涕泣之次' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sudden appearance '忽床下一人，持匕首出立' must be read as a dramatic moment.
  • The reaction '此客大惧' is a direct response to the previous event.
  • The dialogue '我，义士也。宰使我来取君头' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The realization '适闻说，方知此宰负心，不然枉杀贤士' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The promise '吾义不舍此人也。公且勿睡，少顷与君取此宰头，以雪公冤' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The reaction '此人怕惧愧谢' is a direct response to the previous event.
  • The action '剑客持剑，出门如飞' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '二更已至，呼曰：“贼首至”' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '命火观之，乃令头也' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '剑客辞诀，不知所之' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
